rulings.Case Title: WENDY HENRIQUEZ VS. SALLY LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Seeking Order Directing The Sf Sheriff'S Dept., Civil Unit, To Return Funds Seized Pursuant To Writ Of Execution; Request For Hearing Date/Default Prove Up
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday September 11, 2018 line 1. DEFENDANT SALLY LIU Notice Of Motion And Motion Seeking Order Directing The Sf Sheriff's Dept., Civil Unit, To Return Funds Seized Pursuant To Writ Of Execution; Request For Hearing Date/Default Prove Up GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to set up a prove-up hearing is DENIED. Prove-up hearings are handled by department 514 and any motion to set a hearing shall be calendared in that department. Motion to Return Seized Funds is GRANTED. Defendant posted a bond. Plaintiff fails to provide any argument or authority for the proposition that Plaintiff is entitled to collect the funds pending appeal where a bond have been posted. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JEAN LIU VS. JUANA INES TELLO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion/Verified Petition Of Plaintiff For Writ Of Mandamus [Ccp 1085]; Declaration Of Jean Liu

rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC HEIGHTS TOWERS LLC VS. GREG HERMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff'S Demand For Inspection Of Real Property And Request For Sanctions

PLAINTIFF PACIFIC HEIGHTS TOWERS LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff'S Demand For Inspection Of Real Property And Request For Sanctions GRANTED, subject to opposition. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JEAN N LEE VS. GEORGE GALARZA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday September 11, 2018 line 4. DEFENDANT GEORGE GALARZA, OLGA GALARZA, ERICA GALARZA, AMMONETTE GALARZA, SAVANNAH MOLANO DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege payment, not service of copies of checks. Contrary to Plaintiff's assertion, paragraph 10 of the operative complaint does not allege that payment was made, it alleges that Notice was served "along with copies of the checks representing the first halves of the relocation payments." = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA VS. TEALL HENDERSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday September 11, 2018 line 5. DEFENDANT TEALL HENDERSON DEMURRER to COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. Factual issues, including those pertaining to the methods of paying rent and past-due rent, are not properly addressed on demurrer. = (501/REQ)
